title: TC001-Successful registration redirects to dashboard
description: Verifies a user can create an account with valid details, accept terms, and is redirected to the dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /register (use exact path http://localhost:3000/register).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/register", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[@id="password-strength"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Projects').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Registration form not found on /register; loading spinner remains visible and no form inputs detected.
- Username input field not present in interactive elements after scrolling and waiting.
- Required form controls (Email, Password, Confirm Password, Terms checkbox, Create Account button) are missing, preventing account creation testing.
- Redirect to /dashboard and presence of 'Projects' could not be verified because account creation could not be attempted.

title: TC002-Registration blocked when confirm password does not match
description: Verifies the user remains on the registration page and sees an error when passwords do not match.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Get Started' button (index 9) to navigate to the registration/registration flow page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/header/div/nav/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(text(),"Password strength")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Passwords do not match').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/register' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Registration form inputs (Username, Email, Password, Confirm Password, Terms checkbox, Create Account button) not found on the /register page.
- Persistent loading spinner visible after multiple waits and a scroll, indicating the registration UI failed to render.
- Interactive elements list for the page contains only navigation/footer links and no input fields corresponding to the registration form.
- Current URL contains '/register' but required form and controls are not present, preventing completion of form validation checks.

title: TC003-Terms must be accepted to create an account
description: Verifies registration is blocked when the terms checkbox is not checked.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /register (use explicit navigate action to http://localhost:3000/register as required).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/register", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Get Started' button to try to reveal or navigate to the registration form (use interactive element index 224).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/header/div/nav/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Get Started' element again to attempt to reveal or navigate to the registration form (use interactive element index 17713).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Wait a short time for any loading spinner to finish, then click 'Get Started' (index 224) to attempt to reveal or navigate to the registration form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/header/div/nav/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[contains(@class,"password-strength") or contains(@id,"password-strength") or contains(text(),"Strength") or contains(text(),"strength")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/register' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Accept').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Registration form inputs (Username, Email, Password, Confirm, Create Account) not found on the /register page.
- Create Account button not visible on the page.
- Terms/Accept text or checkbox not visible on the page.
- Loading spinner present and page appears to still be rendering, preventing form interaction.

title: TC004-Password strength indicator updates for a weak password
description: Verifies the password strength indicator is shown and reflects a weak password entry.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /register to open the registration page so the form and password strength indicator can be tested.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/register", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"password-strength") or contains(@aria-label,"Password strength") or contains(@id,"password-strength")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Weak').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/register' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Registration form not present on the /register page and inputs are not visible.
- Loading spinner persists and blocks access to the registration form after multiple waits.
- No username, email, password, or confirm-password input elements were found on the page.
- Password strength indicator cannot be verified because the registration form did not render.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000

        # -> Click the 'Create Your Account' link to navigate to the registration page.

        # Click element

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]

title: TC008-Dashboard shows project list for authenticated user
description: Verifies an authenticated user can reach the dashboard and see the projects list UI.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use the explicit navigate action to http://localhost:3000/login)

        # -> Click the top navigation 'Sign in' link to attempt to render the login form and reveal username/password inputs.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element

    finally:

        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (explicit navigation step provided in test plan).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link in the page header (interactive element [286]) to try to reveal the login form fields
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/header/div/nav/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Chat').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

- Persistent central loading spinner is visible and blocks interaction, preventing the login form from rendering.
- Unable to complete login or reach the dashboard, so subsequent workspace and UI visibility checks cannot be performed.

title: TC023-Workspace loads without errors when interacting with multiple panels sequentially
description: Verifies basic stability: user can click a file, switch a chat tab, and toggle visibility without leaving the workspace.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use the required explicit navigation to http://localhost:3000/login)
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        assert '/workspace/123' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
